channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: What happens if Mitch McConnell is unable to finish his US Senate term?
item.description: The longtime party leader's absence from the Senate is likely to have repercussions for the Republican agenda.

item.title: Data breach reportedly targets India’s Kudankulam nuclear power plant
item.description: Purported blueprints of parts of India's largest plant exposed by World Leaks ransomware group, Reuters reports.

item.title: Has Bali’s Melukat ritual turned into a commodified religious ceremony?
item.description: With the world in chaos, many travellers are seeking healing experiences during the summer holidays.

item.title: World Cup: Why is there a third-place playoff between France and England?
item.description: When was it introduced, does it have an impact on the Golden Boot race and what do the managers think?

item.title: How World Cup football jerseys became political fashion in Latin America
item.description: In both Brazil and Colombia, far-right political movements have coopted the national football team's jersey. But why?

item.title: Epstein linked to Israeli ‘deep state’, US intel, says Vance: What we know
item.description: In podcast interview, Vance also acknowledges Trump administration 'screwed up' on communications around Epstein files.

item.title: US or Iran — Who needs a new truce deal more?
item.description: Both sides face pressure points that could make a protracted war painful.

item.title: Sudanese minister says war has ‘profoundly reshaped’ nation’s demographics
item.description: Sudan faces a population shift as conflict empties areas, burdens cities, and disrupts economic and social stability.

item.title: Key moments from JD Vance sit down with Joe Rogan
item.description: Key moments from JD Vance sit down with Joe Rogan

item.title: ‘Falklands are Argentinian’: Celebrations over England win revive old wounds
item.description: The Falkland Islands have been at the heart of a long-running dispute between the United Kingdom and Argentina.

item.title: Powerful storm floods hundreds of homes in Russia’s urals
item.description: Powerful storm floods hundreds of homes in Russia's urals

item.title: US hosts global meet on ‘far-left terror’: Who’s attending, why it matters
item.description: More than 65 countries expected to address 'renewed threats' from 'far-left political violence and terrorism'.

item.title: John Esposito, pioneering scholar of Islam in the US, dies aged 86
item.description: Georgetown scholar and author spent decades building bridges between the Islamic and Western worlds.

item.title: Palestinians in Gaza search through homes destroyed by Israeli strikes
item.description: Palestinians are inspecting the damage in Nuseirat after Israeli strikes reduced their homes to rubble.

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Biggest takeaways from the semifinals
item.description: The semifinals are now behind us, with plenty of key moments and incidents to digest ahead of Sunday's final.

item.title: Thousands to lose their homes as Ivory Coast demolitions begin
item.description: Thousands to lose their homes as Ivory Coast demolitions begin

item.title: Iran claims strikes on US bases, warns of wider attacks on region
item.description: Iran claims strikes on US bases, warns of wider attacks on region

item.title: EU court upholds Spanish amnesty law as compatible with EU law
item.description: Amnesty for people involved in Catalonia independence movement introduced in 2024 by PM Pedro Sanchez's government.

item.title: Venezuela’s recovery clouded by uncertainty three weeks after earthquake
item.description: In Venezuela, the focus has shifted from rescue to rebuilding three weeks after devastating earthquakes.

item.title: ‘Destroyed my whole life’: Lebanese families wiped out in Israeli attacks
item.description: Hussein Saleh describes an Israeli air attack in Tyre that killed his family, including his wife and his only child.

item.title: Palestinians fight fires after settler attacks in the occupied West Bank
item.description: Overnight Israeli settler attacks set farmlands ablaze in Masafer Yatta and burned vehicles near Tulkarem.

item.title: World Cup: Tuchel, Messi and why Argentina beat England in semifinal
item.description: England's poor defensive tactics, Lionel Messi's enduring brilliance, and Argentina self-belief behind 2-1 comeback win.

item.title: Hundreds protest in Kyiv over Zelenskyy’s dismissal of defence minister
item.description: The Ukrainian parliament has elected Serhii Koretskyi as the country's new prime minister.

item.title: Fire at orphanage in Algeria kills 11 people, including children
item.description: Eleven people died and 19 were injured after a fire swept through a state-run child welfare home in east Algiers.

item.title: Climate funding: Who pays for the transition?
item.description: Developed nations are cutting climate funding, but developing countries still need billions to adapt.
